clusters.title: Pentagon restrictions on press violate First Amendment, judge rules

items.content: The Department of Defense had required reporters to agree to certain rules on what information they could gather in order to maintain access to the Pentagon.

items.title: US judge strikes down Pentagon press limits as unconstitutional
items.standfirst: A US judge on Friday ruled that the Pentagon’s controversial media access policy violated constitutional protections, restoring journalists’ credentials after major outlets refused to comply. The decision marks a significant victory for press freedom, underscoring the public’s right to diverse reporting during wartime.
items.content: A US judge on Friday ruled that the Pentagon’s controversial media access policy violated constitutional protections, restoring journalists’ credentials after major outlets refused to comply. items.title: U.S. judge rules against Pentagon restrictions on press coverage
